Name: Exeggcute
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its six eggs use telepathy to communicate among themselves. It is believed to carry plant genes and the genes of other species.

Name: Yanma
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: If it flaps its wings really fast, it can generate shock waves that will shatter windows in the area.

Name: Yanmega
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: This six-legged Pokémon is easily capable of transporting an adult in flight. The wings on its tail help it stay balanced.

Name: Roselia
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads. This Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Roselia that drink nutritionally rich springwater are said to reveal rare coloration when they bloom.

Name: Roserade
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Move any number of [G] Energy from your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: With the movements of a dancer, it strikes with whips that are densely lined with poison thorns.

Name: Turtwig
Effects: Search your deck for a [G] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It undertakes photosynthesis with its body, making oxygen. The leaf on its head wilts if it is thirsty.

Name: Turtwig
Flavor-Text: It undertakes photosynthesis with its body, making oxygen. The leaf on its head wilts if it is thirsty.

Name: Grotle
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It knows where pure water wells up. It carries fellow Pokémon there on its back.

Name: Torterra
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
This attack does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Small Pokémon occasionally gather on its unmoving back to begin building their nests.

Name: Cherubi
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It evolves by sucking the energy out of the small ball where it had been storing nutrients.

Name: Cherrim
Ability-Effect: Your [G] Pokémon have no Weakness.
Flavor-Text: If it senses strong sunlight, it opens its folded petals to absorb the sun’s rays with its whole body.

Name: Carnivine
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
This attack does 10 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It binds itself to trees in marshes. It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling drool and gulps them down.

Name: Leafeon-GX
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, once during your turn (before your attack), you may heal 50 damage from 1 of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it.
Effects: For each of your Benched Basic Pokémon, search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mow Rotom
Ability-Effect: If you have 9 or more Pokémon Tool cards in your discard pile, ignore all Energy in the attack cost of each of this Pokémon’s attacks.
Effects: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.

Name: Shaymin
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon of different types and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.

Name: Dewpider
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It crawls onto the land in search of food. Its water bubble allows it to breathe and protects its soft head.

Name: Araquanid
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s [R] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It delivers headbutts with the water bubble on its head. Small Pokémon get sucked into the bubble, where they drown.

Name: Magmar
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When angered, it spouts brilliant fire from all over its body. It doesn’t calm down until its opponent has burned to ash.

Name: Magmortar
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Burned.
Effects: You may discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: From its arm, it launches fireballs hotter than 3,500 degrees Fahrenheit. Its arm starts to melt when it fires a whole barrage.

Name: Chimchar
Flavor-Text: The gas made in its belly burns from its rear end. The fire burns weakly when it feels sick.

Name: Chimchar
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The gas made in its belly burns from its rear end. The fire burns weakly when it feels sick.

Name: Monferno
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: It uses ceilings and walls to launch aerial attacks. Its fiery tail is but one weapon.

Name: Infernape
Ability-Effect: Put 6 damage counters instead of 2 on your opponent’s Burned Pokémon between turns.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: It tosses its enemies around with agility. It uses all its limbs to fight in its own unique style.

Name: Heat Rotom
Ability-Effect: If you have 9 or more Pokémon Tool cards in your discard pile, ignore all Energy in the attack cost of each of this Pokémon’s attacks.
Flavor-Text: Its body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.

Name: Salandit
Flavor-Text: Volcanoes or dry, craggy places are its home. It emanates a sweet-smelling poisonous gas that attracts bug Pokémon, then attacks them.

Name: Salazzle
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned, Confused, and Poisoned.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Filled with pheromones, its poisonous gas can be diluted to use in the production of luscious perfumes.

Name: Turtonator
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: It gushes fire and poisonous gases from its nostrils. Its dung is an explosive substance and can be put to various uses.

Name: Alolan Sandshrew
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: An ancient tradition of Alolan festivals, still carried on to this day, is a competition to slide Sandshrew across ice as far as one can.

Name: Alolan Sandslash
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 6 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s steel spikes are sheathed in ice. Stabs from these spikes cause deep wounds and severe frostbite as well.

Name: Alolan Vulpix
Effects: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It exhales air colder than -58 degrees Fahrenheit. Elderly people in Alola call this Pokémon by an older name—Keokeo.

Name: Piplup
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Because it is very proud, it hates accepting food from people. Its thick down guards it from cold.

Name: Piplup
Flavor-Text: Because it is very proud, it hates accepting food from people. Its thick down guards it from cold.

Name: Prinplup
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It lives a solitary life. Its wings deliver wicked blows that can snap even the thickest of trees.

Name: Empoleon
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s).
Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The three horns that extend from its beak attest to its power. The leader has the biggest horns.

Name: Buizel
Flavor-Text: It inflates the flotation sac around its neck and pokes its head out of the water to see what is going on.

Name: Floatzel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Discard a [W] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its flotation sac developed as a result of pursuing aquatic prey. It can double as a rubber raft.

Name: Snover
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a [F] Pokémon, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: In the spring, it grows berries with the texture of frozen treats around its belly.

Name: Abomasnow
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may attach a [W] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It lives a quiet life on mountains that are perpetually covered in snow. It hides itself by whipping up blizzards.

Name: Glaceon-GX
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent’s Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX in play, in their hand, and in their discard pile have no Abilities, except for Freezing Gaze.
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This attack does 50 damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Wash Rotom
Ability-Effect: If you have 9 or more Pokémon Tool cards in your discard pile, ignore all Energy in the attack cost of each of this Pokémon’s attacks.
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.

Name: Frost Rotom
Ability-Effect: If you have 9 or more Pokémon Tool cards in your discard pile, ignore all Energy in the attack cost of each of this Pokémon’s attacks.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.

Name: Manaphy
Effects: Shuffle 5 [W] Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It starts its life with a wondrous power that permits it to bond with any kind of Pokémon.

Name: Electabuzz
Effects: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Electricity leaks from it in amounts far greater than the amount of electricity it eats.

Name: Electivire
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a [M] Pokémon, it is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: When it gets excited, it thumps its chest. With every thud, thunder roars and electric sparks shower all around.

Name: Shinx
Ability-Effect: If you go second, this Pokémon can evolve during your first turn.
Flavor-Text: All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

Name: Shinx
Effects: Search your deck for a [L] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

Name: Luxio
Effects: Your opponent can’t play any Item cards from their hand during their next turn.
Flavor-Text: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

Name: Luxray
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Discard all [L] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 150 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Luxray’s ability to see through objects comes in handy when it’s scouting for danger.

Name: Pachirisu
Effects: For each of your Benched Pokémon that has the Nuzzle attack, search your deck for a [L] Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

Name: Rotom
Ability-Effect: If you have 9 or more Pokémon Tool cards in your discard pile, ignore all Energy in the attack cost of each of this Pokémon’s attacks.
Effects: This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: Its body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.

Name: Drifloon
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: If for some reason its body bursts, its soul spills out with a screaming sound.

Name: Drifblim
Effects: Move 4 damage counters from each of your Pokémon to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Even while under careful observation, large flocks of Drifblim flying at dusk will inexplicably disappear from view.

Name: Spiritomb
Effects: Put 2 Supporter cards from your discard pile into your hand.
If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It was bound to a fissure in an Odd Keystone as punishment for misdeeds 500 years ago.

Name: Skorupi
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Pierce attack’s base damage is 90.
Flavor-Text: It burrows under the sand to lie in wait for prey. Its tail claws can inject its prey with a savage poison.

Name: Drapion
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It has the power in its clawed arms to make scrap of a car. The tips of its claws release poison.

Name: Croagunk
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

Name: Toxicroak
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
If any of your [F] Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack during their last turn, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its knuckle claws secrete a toxin so vile that even a scratch could prove fatal.

Name: Giratina Prism Star
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may attach 2 [P] Energy cards from your hand to it.
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Prism-Star-Rule: You can’t have more than 1 [*] card with the same name in your deck. If a [*] card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.
Flavor-Text: It was banished for its violence. It silently gazed upon the old world from the Distortion World.

Name: Cresselia
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, switch all damage counters on this Pokémon with those on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Those who sleep holding Cresselia’s feather are assured of joyful dreams. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

Name: Cosmog
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its body is gaseous and frail. It slowly grows as it collects dust from the atmosphere.

Name: Cosmoem
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: There’s something accumulating around the black core within its hard shell. People think this Pokémon may come from another world.

Name: Lunala Prism Star
Effects: For each of your opponent’s Pokémon in play, attach a [P] Energy card from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.
This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all Pokémon.
Prism-Star-Rule: You can’t have more than 1 [*] card with the same name in your deck. If a [*] card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.
Flavor-Text: Said to live in another world, this Pokémon devours light, drawing the moonless dark veil of night over the brightness of day.

Name: Dawn Wings Necrozma-GX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.

You can use this attack only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent. Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Cranidos
Flavor-Text: In rock layers where Cranidos fossils are found, the fossilized trunks of trees snapped in two are also often found.

Name: Rampardos
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it is Knocked Out.
Flavor-Text: Records exist of a revived fossil that evolved into Rampardos. It proceeded to escape and then destroy a skyscraper with a headbutt.

Name: Riolu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It’s tough enough to run right through the night, and it’s also a hard worker, but it’s still just a youngster.

Name: Riolu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It’s tough enough to run right through the night, and it’s also a hard worker, but it’s still just a youngster.

Name: Lucario
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Garchomp in play, you may search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: They can detect the species of a living being—and its emotions—from over half a mile away. They control auras and hunt their prey in packs.

Name: Lucario
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Garchomp in play, you may search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: They can detect the species of a living being—and its emotions—from over half a mile away. They control auras and hunt their prey in packs.

Name: Hippopotas
Effects: If there are 3 or fewer cards in your deck, this attack does 130 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It enshrouds itself with sand to protect itself from germs. It does not enjoy getting wet.

Name: Hippowdon
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
This attack does 10 more damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: It blasts internally stored sand from ports on its body to create a towering twister for attack.

Name: Passimian
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, your Passimian’s attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Evolution Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: They battle with hard berries for weapons. Their techniques are passed from the boss to the group, generation upon generation.

Name: Murkrow
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Seen as a symbol of bad luck, it’s generally disliked. Yet it gives presents—objects that sparkle or shine—to Trainers it’s close to.

Name: Honchkrow
Effects: Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If its Murkrow cronies fail to catch food for it, or if it feels they have betrayed it, it will hunt them down wherever they are and punish them.

Name: Sneasel
Effects: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only on your first turn. Discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It uses its claws to poke holes in eggs so it can slurp out the insides. Breeders consider it a scourge and will drive it away or eradicate it.

Name: Weavile
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
This attack does 50 damage for each of your opponent’s Pokémon that has an Ability.
Flavor-Text: They dwell in cold places. This Pokémon’s main food source in Alola is Vulpix and Sandshrew, which they carefully divide among their group.

Name: Stunky
Effects: Both Active Pokémon are now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It protects itself by spraying a noxious fluid from its rear. The stench lingers for 24 hours.

Name: Skuntank
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips 2 coins. If either of them is tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It sprays a stinky fluid from its tail. The fluid smells worse the longer it is allowed to fester.

Name: Darkrai Prism Star
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may attach 2 [D] Energy cards from your hand to it.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep. Your opponent flips 2 coins instead of 1 between turns. If either of them is tails, that Pokémon is still Asleep.
Prism-Star-Rule: You can’t have more than 1 [*] card with the same name in your deck. If a [*] card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.
Flavor-Text: It can lull people to sleep and make them dream. It is active during nights of the new moon.

Name: Alolan Diglett
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its golden hairs function as sensors. It pokes them out of its burrow to monitor its surroundings.

Name: Alolan Dugtrio
Effects: Discard any number of [M] Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 30 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon are cherished in the Alola region, where they are thought to be feminine deities of the land incarnate.

Name: Magnemite
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 [M] Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It sends out electromagnetic waves, which let it float through the air. Touching it while it’s eating electricity will give you a full-body shock.

Name: Magnemite
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 [M] Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It sends out electromagnetic waves, which let it float through the air. Touching it while it’s eating electricity will give you a full-body shock.

Name: Magnemite
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).
Flavor-Text: It sends out electromagnetic waves, which let it float through the air. Touching it while it’s eating electricity will give you a full-body shock.

Name: Magneton
Effects: This Pokémon can’t use Zap Cannon during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: It has about three times the electrical power of Magnemite. For some reason, outbreaks of this Pokémon happen when lots of sunspots appear.

Name: Magneton
Effects: This Pokémon can’t use Zap Cannon during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: It has about three times the electrical power of Magnemite. For some reason, outbreaks of this Pokémon happen when lots of sunspots appear.

Name: Magnezone
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a [M] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Effects: This Pokémon can’t use Zap Cannon during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: As it zooms through the sky, this Pokémon seems to be receiving signals of unknown origin, while transmitting signals of unknown purpose.

Name: Magnezone
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a [M] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Effects: This Pokémon can’t use Zap Cannon during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: As it zooms through the sky, this Pokémon seems to be receiving signals of unknown origin, while transmitting signals of unknown purpose.

Name: Shieldon
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lived in primeval jungles. Few enemies would have been willing to square off against its heavily armored face, so it’s thought.

Name: Bastiodon
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to your [M] Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon that have any Special Energy attached to them.
Effects: You may have your opponent switch their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lived in the same environments as Rampardos. Their fossils have been found together—seemingly from after they’d fought to the finish.

Name: Bronzor
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Implements shaped like it were discovered in ancient tombs. It is unknown if they are related.

Name: Bronzong
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
If your opponent has any [P] Pokémon in play, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Ancient people believed that petitioning Bronzong for rain was the way to make crops grow.

Name: Heatran
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Boiling blood, like magma, circulates through its body. It makes its dwelling place in volcanic caves.

Name: Solgaleo Prism Star
Effects: For each of your opponent’s Pokémon in play, attach a [M] Energy card from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.
This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
Prism-Star-Rule: You can’t have more than 1 [*] card with the same name in your deck. If a [*] card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is said to be a male evolution of Cosmog. At the activation of its third eye, it departs for another world.

Name: Dusk Mane Necrozma-GX
Effects: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.
You can use this attack only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Magearna
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put a Pokémon Tool card attached to 1 of your Pokémon into your hand.
Flavor-Text: This artificial Pokémon, constructed more than 500 years ago, can understand human speech but cannot itself speak.

Name: Morelull
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It scatters spores that flicker and glow. Anyone seeing these lights falls into a deep slumber.

Name: Shiinotic
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a [Y] Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Forests where Shiinotic live are treacherous to enter at night. People confused by its strange lights can never find their way home again.

Name: Tapu Lele
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Move any number of damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon to their other Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: This guardian deity of Akala is guilelessly cruel. The fragrant aroma of flowers is the source of its energy.

Name: Alolan Exeggutor
Effects: For each of your Benched Exeggcute, search your deck for an Alolan Exeggutor or Alolan Exeggutor-GX and put it onto that Exeggcute to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flip a coin for each [G] Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Alola is the best environment for this Pokémon. Local people take pride in its appearance, saying this is how Exeggutor ought to look.

Name: Gible
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It skulks in caves, and when prey or an enemy passes by, it leaps out and chomps them. The force of its attack sometimes chips its teeth.

Name: Gible
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It skulks in caves, and when prey or an enemy passes by, it leaps out and chomps them. The force of its attack sometimes chips its teeth.

Name: Gible
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [F] Energy attached to it, it has no Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: It skulks in caves, and when prey or an enemy passes by, it leaps out and chomps them. The force of its attack sometimes chips its teeth.

Name: Gabite
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Shiny objects are its passion. It can be found in its cave, scarcely moving, its gaze fixed on the jewels it’s amassed or Carbink it has caught.

Name: Gabite
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Shiny objects are its passion. It can be found in its cave, scarcely moving, its gaze fixed on the jewels it’s amassed or Carbink it has caught.

Name: Garchomp
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If you played Cynthia from your hand during this turn, this attack does 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The protuberances on its head serve as sensors. It can even detect distant prey.

Name: Lickitung
Effects: Draw 3 cards.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its tongue is twice the length of its body. It can be moved like an arm for grabbing food and attacking.

Name: Lickilicky
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 more damage for each heads. If the first flip is tails, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Their saliva contains lots of components that can dissolve anything. The numbness caused by their lick does not dissipate.

Name: Eevee
Flavor-Text: Current studies show it can evolve into an incredible eight different species of Pokémon.

Name: Eevee
Effects: This attack does 10 damage for each different type of Pokémon on your Bench.
Flavor-Text: Possessing an unbalanced and unstable genetic makeup, it conceals many possible evolutions.

Name: Buneary
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: When it senses danger, it perks up its ears. On cold nights, it sleeps with its head tucked into its fur.

Name: Lopunny
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
You may shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.
Flavor-Text: The ears appear to be delicate. If they are touched roughly, it kicks with its graceful legs.

Name: Glameow
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s attacks do 40 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: When it’s happy, Glameow demonstrates beautiful movements of its tail, like a dancing ribbon.

Name: Purugly
Effects: If your opponent has a Stadium card in play, discard it. If you do, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Discard random cards from your opponent’s hand until they have 3 cards in their hand.
Flavor-Text: To make itself appear intimidatingly beefy, it tightly cinches its waist with its twin tails.

Name: Fan Rotom
Ability-Effect: If you have 9 or more Pokémon Tool cards in your discard pile, ignore all Energy in the attack cost of each of this Pokémon’s attacks.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.

Name: Shaymin
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.

Name: Yungoos
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand.
This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It wanders around in a never-ending search for food. At dusk, it collapses from exhaustion and falls asleep on the spot.

Name: Gumshoos
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. If you find a Pokémon there, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It adores having Rattata and Raticate for dinner, but as it’s diurnal, it never encounters them. This Pokémon boasts incredible patience.

Name: Oranguru
Effects: Put 3 cards from your discard pile on the bottom of your deck in any order.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Deep in the jungle, high in the lofty canopy, this Pokémon abides. On rare occasions, it shows up at the beach to match wits with Slowking.

Name: Type: Null
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The heavy control mask it wears suppresses its intrinsic capabilities. This Pokémon has some hidden special power.

GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
